title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They think the thief entered through the ventilation shaft.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive with a perfect infinitive to refer to a past action.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll pay for the broken window,’ said the boy.
questions.explanation: Tests reporting verbs followed by a to-infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: I didn't see the hole, so I tripped and fell.
questions.afterGap: the hole, I wouldn't have tripped and fallen.
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for hypothetical past events.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure it was a shock to hear the news.
questions.afterGap: a shock to hear the news.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for logical deduction about the past.

questions.firstSentence: The door was locked, so I couldn't get in.
questions.explanation: Tests verb + object + preposition + gerund (prevent from -ing).

questions.firstSentence: I haven't been to the cinema for ages.
questions.afterGap: I last went to the cinema.
questions.explanation: Tests time structures with 'since' and the present perfect.
